title: Maa Samleswari Temple
tagline: Maa Samleswari Temple — spiritual town of Odisha

overview.short: Samaleswari Temple is a Hindu temple in Sambalpur, Odisha, India, dedicated to the goddess known as 'Samaleswari', also known among the natives as Samalei Maa, meaning Mother Samal
overview.description: Samaleswari Temple is a Hindu temple in Sambalpur, Odisha, India, dedicated to the goddess known as 'Samaleswari', also known among the natives as Samalei Maa, meaning Mother Samaleswari. Shree Shree Samaleswari, the revived presiding deity of Sambalpur, holds a profound religious significance across western Odisha and Chhattisgarh in India. Situated on the banks of the sacred Mahanadi River, Goddess Samaleswari has been worshipped since ancient times as Jagat Janani (Mother of the Universe), Durga, Mahalaxmi, Mahasaraswati, and Adishakti. After Lord Jagannath, she is the only deity in Odisha

topPlaces.name: Anantasaayi Vishnu Temple
topPlaces.description: The Anantasaayi Vishnu temple is located in Sambalpur. It is famous for the black chlorite image of resting Vishnu.

topPlaces.name: Sambalpur State
topPlaces.description: Sambalpur State, also known as Hirakhand Kingdom, was a kingdom founded in 1570. It ruled over Western Odisha and Eastern Chhattisgarh in central-eastern India prior to the Maratha occupation in 1800 AD. From 1849 AD, it was integrated with British Raj as a British District.

topPlaces.name: Sambalpur
topPlaces.description: Sambalpur (Odia: [sɔmbɔlɔpuɾɔ] ) is the fifth-largest city in the Indian State of Odisha. It is located on the banks of river Mahanadi, with a population of 335,761 (as per 2011 census).

topPlaces.name: Sitalsasthi
topPlaces.description: Shitalasasthi, also known as Sital Sasthi, is a Hindu festival that celebrates the wedding of the deities Shiva and Parvati. It is primarily observed by the Utkal Brahmins (commonly referred to as Odia Brahmins) and Aranyaka Brahmins (also known as Jhadua Brahmins).

topPlaces.name: Veer Surendra Sai Museum
topPlaces.description: Veer Surendra Sai Museum (Odia:ବୀର ସୁରେନ୍ଦ୍ର ସାଇ ସଂଗ୍ରହାଳୟ; trans. Bīra Surendra Sāi Saṅgrahāḷaya), formerly known as Victoria Town Hall, is a cultural and historical museum in Sambalpur, Odisha.

topPlaces.name: Budharaja
topPlaces.description: Budharaja is a small hillock in Sambalpur, Odisha, India. There is a Shiva temple situated on the top of hill and middle of the sambalpur city.

topPlaces.name: Roman Catholic Diocese of Sambalpur
topPlaces.description: The Roman Catholic Diocese of Sambalpur (Latin: Sambalpuren(sis)) is a diocese located in the city of Sambalpur in the ecclesiastical province of Cuttack-Bhubaneswar in India.

topPlaces.name: Veer Surendra Sai Stadium
topPlaces.description: Veer Surendra Sai Stadium is a cricket stadium in Sambalpur, Odisha. The ground is named after Surendra Sai, an Indian freedom fighter who died fighting the British.

items.note: The Anantasaayi Vishnu temple is located in Sambalpur. It is famous for the black chlorite image of resting Vishnu.

Sambalpur State, also known as Hirakhand Kingdom, was a kingdom founded in 1570. It ruled over Western Odisha and Eastern Chhattisgarh in central-eastern India prior to the Maratha occupation in 1800 AD. From 1849 AD, it was integrated with British Raj as a British District.
Sambalpur (Odia: [sɔmbɔlɔpuɾɔ] ) is the fifth-largest city in the Indian State of Odisha. It is located on the banks of river Mahanadi, with a population of 335,761 (as per 2011 census).

Shitalasasthi, also known as Sital Sasthi, is a Hindu festival that celebrates the wedding of the deities Shiva and Parvati. It is primarily observed by the Utkal Brahmins (commonly referred to as Odia Brahmins) and Aranyaka Brahmins (also known as Jhadua Brahmins).
Veer Surendra Sai Museum (Odia:ବୀର ସୁରେନ୍ଦ୍ର ସାଇ ସଂଗ୍ରହାଳୟ; trans. Bīra Surendra Sāi Saṅgrahāḷaya), formerly known as Victoria Town Hall, is a cultural and historical museum in Sambalpur, Odisha.
Budharaja is a small hillock in Sambalpur, Odisha, India. There is a Shiva temple situated on the top of hill and middle of the sambalpur city.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Maa Samleswari Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Maa Samleswari Temple is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Maa Samleswari Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Swami Vivekananda Airport, Raipur (232 km) and the nearest railway station is Sambalpur Junction (2 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Maa Samleswari Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Maa Samleswari Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Maa Samleswari Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Maa Samleswari Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Maa Samleswari Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Anantasaayi Vishnu Temple, Sambalpur State, Sambalpur, Sitalsasthi, Veer Surendra Sai Museum.
